zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John St John Sr於1426年1月28日出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾布勒左時，他的父親Sir Oliver St John25歲，母親Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset21歲。他與Lady Alice Bradshaw在英國英格蘭貝德福爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1489年11月29日在英國貝德福爾布勒左享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John St John Sr于1426年1月28日出生在英国英格兰貝德福爾布勒左时，他的父亲Sir Oliver St John25岁，母亲Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset21岁。他与Lady Alice Bradshaw在英国英格兰貝德福爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1489年11月29日在英国貝德福爾布勒左享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Ancar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Alphonse, Etienne, Fabien, Mireille.

Probably an altered form of French (Pas-de-Calais, Nord) Ancart, itself a variant of Hancart and Hanecart: from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Han, a short form of French Jehan and Flemish Johan(nes) (see John ).

variants.name: Bongiovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Antonio, Dante, Salvatore, Santo, Antonino, Domenic, Gaetano, Geno, Giacomo, Gilda, Luciano, Nino.
Italian: from a personal name composed of the elements bon ‘good’ + Giovanni, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: Di Giovanna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Alfonso, Carlo, Domenic, Gaetano, Liborio, Nunzio, Pietro, Rosario, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: metronymic from the female personal name Giovanna, an equivalent of Joan (see John ).

variants.name: Anes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Angel, Dionisio, Esperanza, Gilberto, Ines, Juanita, Luis, Luisa, Manuel, Manuela, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Burhans
meanings: German: from Middle Low German būr ‘farmer, peasant’ + the personal name Hans, a short form of Johannes (see John ), hence a distinguishing occupational name ‘farmer John’ with Hans being a very frequent name. See also Burian .

variants.name: Breihan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt.
German: distinguishing nickname for a (beer) brewer, from Middle High German briuw(er) ‘brew(er)’ + a short form of the personal name Johann(es) (see John ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Middle English male personal name Jan(e), from Old French Jehan, a variant of Johan, which gave rise to the common alternative John . Sometimes the name may also arise from the Welsh variant Ieun of Welsh Ieuan, likewise equivalent to John (compare Evan ).

variants.name: Di Giovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Vito, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo, Gino, Giuseppe, Guido, Agostino, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Giovanni, an equivalent of John . Compare De Giovanni , Dejohn , and Giovanni .

variants.name: Eanes
meanings: English (Hampshire): probably a variant of Heanes with loss of initial H-, itself a variant of Heane, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a nickname from Middle English hen(e), heine ‘poor, needy, wretched’ (Old English hēan). Eanes is now rare in Britain, and in the US is found predominantly in VA.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Ene
meanings: Romanian: from a derivative of the personal name Ioan (see John ).
West African (Nigeria): unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John St John Sr was born on 28 January 1426, in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Oliver St John, was 25 and his mother, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, was 21. He married Lady Alice Bradshaw in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 29 November 1489, in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John St John Sr est né le 28 janvier 1426, à Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Oliver St John, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Lady Alice Bradshaw à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 29 novembre 1489, à Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John St John Sr am 28. Januar 1426 in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Oliver St John 25 und seine Mutter Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset 21. Er hat in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Lady Alice Bradshaw geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 29. November 1489 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John St John Sr è nato il il 28 gennaio 1426 nel Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Oliver St John, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Lady Alice Bradshaw nel Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 29 novembre 1489, nel Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John St John Srが1426年1月28日にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアブレトソで生まれた時，父Sir Oliver St Johnは25歳，母Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somersetは21歳であった。イギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアでLady Alice Bradshawと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1489年11月29日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアブレトソで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John St John Sr(이)가 1426년 1월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 블레츠에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Oliver St John(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset(은)는 21세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 Lady Alice Bradshaw(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1489년 11월 29일 영국 베드퍼드실르 블레츠에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.28 Jan 1426–29 November 1489: 28 de janeiro de 1426–29 de novembro de 1489
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John St John Sr nasceu em 28 de janeiro de 1426, em Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Oliver St John, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Alice Bradshaw em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de novembro de 1489, em Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John St John Sr родился 28 января 1426 г. в местности блецо, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Oliver St John было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice Bradshaw в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 29 ноября 1489 г. в местности блецо, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет.

es.28 Jan 1426–29 November 1489: 28 de enero de 1426–29 de noviembre de 1489
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John St John Sr nació el 28 de enero de 1426, en Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Oliver St John, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Lady Alice Bradshaw en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 29 de noviembre de 1489, en Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años.
